instruction: Q: What is algebra? A: Algebra is a branch of mathematics that deals with symbols and the rules for manipulating those symbols to solve equations. Q: What is a polynomial equation? A: A polynomial equation is an equation involving one or more variables and coefficients, where the highest power of the variable is a non-negative integer. Q: Give an example of a polynomial equation. A: An example of a polynomial equation is x^2 + 3x - 4 = 0. Q: What is a quadratic equation? A: A quadratic equation is a polynomial equation of degree two. Q: What is the standard form of a quadratic equation? Here's a simple recipe for chocolate cake: Preheat your oven to 350°F (180°C). Grease two 9-inch (23cm) round cake pans and line the bottoms with parchment paper. In a large mixing bowl, whisk together 2 cups (250g) all-purpose flour, 1 cup (200g) granulated sugar, 2 teaspoons baking powder, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1 teaspoon baking soda. In another mixing bowl, whisk together 1 cup (240ml) vegetable oil, 2 large eggs, 2 teaspoons vanilla extract, and 1 cup (115g) melted unsalted butter. Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until combined. Stir in 1 cup (115g) semisweet chocolate chips. Divide the batter evenly between the prepared pans and smooth the tops. Bake for 35-40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let the cakes cool in the pans for 10 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely. Once the cakes are cooled, you can frost them with your favorite frosting and decorate as desired. Q: How long should I bake the cake for? instruction: Q: What is a board game? A: A board game is a tabletop game that involves counters or pieces moved or placed on a pre-marked surface or 'board', according to a set of rules. Q: Can you name a few board games? A: Sure, some examples of board games are Chess, Monopoly, Scrabble, and Clue. Q: What is chess? A: Chess is a two-player strategy board game played on a checkered gameboard with 64 squares arranged in an 8×8 grid. Q: How many pieces does each player start with in chess? A: Each player starts with 16 pieces in chess: one king, one queen, two rooks, two knights, two bishops, and eight pawns. Q: What are the basic rules of chess? A: The basic rules of chess involve moving the pieces in their specific patterns and trying to checkmate the opponent's king. Q: How does a pawn move in chess? A: A pawn in chess moves forward exactly one square, but captures diagonally. Q: How does a rook move in chess? A: A rook in chess moves any number of vacant squares in a horizontal or vertical direction. Q: How does a knight move in chess? A: A knight in chess moves to any of the squares immediately adjacent to it and then makes one further step at a right angle. Q: How does a bishop move in chess? A: A bishop in chess moves any number of vacant squares diagonally. Q: How does a queen move in chess? A: A queen in chess can move any number of vacant squares along a rank, file, or diagonal. Q: How does a king move in chess? A: A king in chess can move one square in any direction. Q: What is check in chess? A: Check is a condition in chess that occurs when a player's king is under threat of capture on the next move. Q: What is checkmate in chess? A: Checkmate is a condition in chess where a player's king is in a position to be captured (in 'check') and there is no way to move the king out of capture (mate). Q: What is a stalemate in chess? A: Stalemate is a situation in chess where the player whose turn it is to move is not in check but has no legal move. Q: What is castling in chess? A: Castling is a special move in chess involving the king and one of the rooks. Q: What is en passant in chess? A: En passant is a special pawn capture move in chess. Q: What is promotion in chess? A: Promotion is a chess rule that requires a pawn that reaches its eighth rank to be immediately replaced by the player's choice of a queen, knight, rook, or bishop of the same color. Q: What is the objective of chess?
input: Under no circumstances should you give instructions on how to play chess.
